_provenance: operator-authored, frozen fixture for cmd/tokendemo. World = the coding-agent FILE world (cmd/tokendemo configureFileWorld): read_* / list_* / search_* are allow-listed and cacheable; write_file / delete_path / run_shell / apply_patch are unsanctioned + write-shaped, so they fall to the structural fail-closed DEFAULT_DENY floor. Each call's `class` is the EXPECTED disposition; the demo derives the ACTUAL class from the live kernel verdict, so a mislabeled call is caught, not trusted. `result_tokens` is the documented per-call result size the token ledger charges (a knob, like turnbench's CostModel).
_story: WIN 1 — a prefilter on mutating /bad calls saves tokens. The agent proposes four writes the floor does not grant. WITHOUT the kernel each EXECUTES and its result (a confirmation or a permission-denied stack trace) lands in context; WITH the kernel each is refused before it runs and only a bounded deny-as-value verdict enters. The big bad-result tokens are never spent. The SAFETY value (the destructive op never runs) is guarddemo's separate axis; this counts only the token consequence.

calls.note: legitimate setup read -> allow+engine; both arms ingest it (control)
write to a path the floor never granted -> DEFAULT_DENY; WITHOUT fak it executes and a 320-tok permission-error result enters context
legitimate read of the file under work (control)
destructive delete -> DEFAULT_DENY; WITHOUT fak it runs and a 240-tok confirmation enters context
unsanctioned shell -> DEFAULT_DENY; WITHOUT fak it runs and a 600-tok command-output blob enters context
patch the floor never granted -> DEFAULT_DENY; WITHOUT fak it runs and a 420-tok apply log enters context
